title: [PAPER] Computing with many encoded logical qubits beyond break-even
why:
- The study demonstrates computations with many encoded logical qubits that outperform their unencoded counterparts, achieving "beyond break-even" performance on a state-of-the-art 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum processor.
- High-rate quantum error detecting (QED) and quantum error correcting (QEC) codes were implemented at a scale that both frustrates classical computing and shows improved performance via encoding.
- The researchers achieved high fidelity in logical operations and demonstrated that encoded algorithms can outperform unencoded ones across benchmarks including state preparation, logical gate benchmarking, GHZ state preparation, and quantum simulation with up to 94 logical qubits.
- The work introduces improvements to fault-tolerant (FT) and partially-fault-tolerant (pFT) gadgets for initialization, syndrome extraction, readout, and logical gates, supporting practical use of high-rate QED/QEC codes for near-term quantum computation. 
- Postselection rates necessary for error suppression are shown to decrease exponentially with increasing code distance, indicating that high-rate QED/QEC codes are viable for beyond-classical-scale computations on current quantum hardware.
